destinations.evidence: Scheduled synthesis now skips on active-job read failures, and the regular async commitment path pre-checks parent-state active jobs before building a commitment, marking pool-sourced duplicates invalid on active jobs or lookup errors.

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port: synthesized scheduled transactions fail closed on active-job lookup errors, and the regular async commitment path now checks parent-state AsyncJobTracker active-job state before commitment construction. Active existing jobs and lookup failures skip the original async candidate and mark pool-sourced transactions invalid; inactive/expired jobs are still allowed to proceed, matching the current downstream expiry-aware behavior.

related_but_insufficient_code: Before the local fix, the scheduled path skipped only successful active-job detections and the regular async path relied on verifier protection. The builder now performs the active-job precheck before regular commitment construction.

rationale: The source commit describes the pool update race and introduced parent-state job existence checks before adding detected async transactions. Later code made the check expiry-aware via job_exists_and_active, so expired jobs may be re-committed while active jobs and lookup failures still skip conservatively. The stable invariant is using synchronous parent contract state, rather than async pool state, to avoid duplicate active commitments.
statement: When building a block, scheduled and regular async transactions must be skipped if their tx hash already exists as an active, non-expired job in parent-state AsyncJobTracker, or if the contract-state active-job check fails; pool-sourced candidates should also be marked invalid so the builder does not repeatedly attempt the same duplicate commitment.
suggested_falsification_tests: Set parent AsyncJobTracker.jobExecutor[tx_hash] and active expiry state for a scheduled and regular async transaction, then run payload building; both should be skipped and not produce async commitments. Also force the active-job read to fail and assert the transaction is skipped, with pool-sourced candidates marked invalid.
summary: Duplicate active-job builder skip behavior is mapped in the port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Preload active AsyncJobTracker state for scheduled and regular async candidates, then build a payload; the mapped port path should skip both, mark pool-sourced candidates invalid, and not produce duplicate commitments.
title: Builder skips commitments for existing jobs
